reviews.review_text:
[h1] THE BIRTH OF A PUZZLE MASTERPIECE [/h1]

[b][i]  A short, brilliant, and absolutely flawless puzzle game that completely redefined first-person mechanics. Armed with nothing but a portal gun, navigating the sterile test chambers of Aperture Science feels incredibly rewarding as the physics-based puzzles constantly challenge your spatial awareness.

The game’s true genius lies in its dark, cynical humor and the introduction of GLaDOS, one of the most iconic and brilliantly written antagonists in video game history. A tight, perfectly paced masterpiece with zero filler.

The cake is a lie. 9.5/10  [/i][/b]

After buying Portal I did something unforgivable. Backlog. I don't even know why - I didn't have any bias towards it, I just would rather play something... Different. 
So imagine my surpise when I realised I am REALLY enjoying this game. I loved snarky android comments, puzzles were really simple (they could be a little more difficult), but they were a perfect mix of logic (but I wish that the difficulty of puzzles was higher) and gameplay, which is shooting and parkour. Oh and I absolutely vibed to end credits song. 
It's a pretty short game, however you can squeze every inch of it by doing achievements or at least trying yourself at challenges. 
As for achievements, they were alright - most of them were easy, however some people might struggle to get gold medals, but with a little practise (and maybe a tutorial) everything is achieveable.
Overall, I have enjoyed my portal experience and together with my lover we're going straight to Portal 2.

[i]portal feels like a dream designed by a machine that doesn’t understand dreams.[/i]

portal is described as a puzzle game first. a clever game. a physics game. a game about momentum and impossible architecture. but none of those descriptions ever fully capture the strange emotional silence sitting underneath it. the mechanics are only the surface layer—clean white walls, portals opening like wounds in reality, test chambers arranged with obsessive precision. underneath that is something colder. something lonely.

it's not just about using a weapon to create 'doors' on walls while listening to a robot’s voice. portal is an experience of solitude where you wake up in an unknown place, voiceless, listening to a synthetic artificial construct trying to imitate human behavior. everything in the game feels manufactured to resemble comfort without actually understanding it. the clean rooms. the calm instructions. the jokes. the fake politeness. it all feels like a machine attempting to reconstruct humanity from observation alone. and that’s what makes the atmosphere so unsettling.

most games want you to feel important. portal barely acknowledges you as a person. you are simply moved from room to room like an object inside a laboratory. nobody asks who you are. nobody explains why you’re there. there are no emotional introductions, no dramatic speeches, no attempts to build attachment. the game trusts emptiness more than exposition. story makes sense in its emptiness—and it does not.

the narrative is fragmented in a way that feels accidental, yet perfectly intentional. you collect pieces of meaning through abandoned corners, hidden graffiti, strange glitches in the environment, moments where the sterile perfection of the facility begins to rot at the edges. you start realizing that the world outside your immediate path exists, but only barely. like memories erased halfway through deletion.

the abandoned maintenance rooms are probably more disturbing than anything explicitly horrific in modern games. not because they contain gore or monsters, but because they contain traces of human presence. tiny signs that somebody else was once trapped here too. scribbled warnings. makeshift living spaces hidden behind the walls. desperation preserved in silence. those moments completely change the tone of the game. suddenly aperture science no longer feels futuristic—it feels haunted.

GLaDOS. she is one of the few antagonists in gaming who feels terrifying without ever raising her voice. her dialogue sounds emotionally detached, but underneath it there’s this constant passive cruelty. the way she speaks feels less like a villain threatening you and more like a system casually observing your suffering as data. sometimes she sounds amused. sometimes almost curious. sometimes strangely lonely herself.

that’s what makes her memorable. she doesn’t feel evil in a traditional sense. she feels artificial in the most believable way possible.

portal understands that isolation becomes stronger when there is only one voice left in the world.

the soundtrack also deserves more credit for this feeling. most of the game is nearly silent. you hear mechanical hums, distant machinery, the echo of your own footsteps. music only appears in fragments, and because of that, every sound matters. the emptiness becomes part of the storytelling. silence in portal is not absence—it’s architecture.

even humor in portal feels cold.

the jokes are funny, but there’s always something uncomfortable attached to them. the iconic cake line became a meme large enough to escape the game itself, but inside the actual experience, it doesn’t feel random or quirky. it feels desperate. the humor acts like a coping mechanism for both the player and the machine speaking to them.

and maybe that’s why the game still stays with people after finishing it.

not because of the puzzles alone.

not because of the portal mechanic.

but because of a feeling very few games can replicate: being completely alone inside a place built for human beings by something that no longer understands what being human means.

the game never tries to emotionally manipulate you. it never forces sadness. it never explains its themes directly. it simply places you inside silence and lets the atmosphere slowly dissolve into your thoughts.

portal feels empty in the same way abandoned laboratories feel empty.

not dead.

just left behind.

PEAK.I expected a simple puzzle game but i was pleasantly surprised. This game is so unique and full of personality despite literally being a white box.It's also amazing game play wise, it feels amazing to figure out the solution and even more amazing when you cheese it! The advanced levels really rack your brain and force you to be smart with how you time portals.The ending is one I'll never forget :)

I come back to Portal twice a year or so to replay it. It's never boring to revisit it, and I'll never forget my first playthrough. It's not a long game, but what it does in the short runtime is incredible.

[h1]Portal gun go pew[/h1]

Even after 18 years, Portal still feels surprisingly fresh. The gameplay is simple to understand but incredibly satisfying, and using the portal gun to solve increasingly clever test chambers never gets old. Some of the puzzles can be frustrating at times, but figuring them out always feels rewarding.

I expected the achievements to be much harder to complete, but they ended up being really enjoyable with just the right amount of challenge. The bonus challenge levels are also a lot of fun and add some nice replay value.

What really makes the game stand out is GLaDOS. Her dark humour and constant commentary make an already great puzzle game even more memorable. Short, sweet, perfectly paced, and without a second of wasted time.

[spoiler]R.I.P. Companion Cube (Test Chamber 17 – Test Chamber 17) — you will be missed.[/spoiler]

[i]The cake is a lie.[/i] 9.2/10

Portal is an elegantly designed puzzler with the now iconic titular portal mechanic. The game does a fantastic job of explaining the main mechanic and slowly ramping up the complexity of each puzzle. The introduction is so well done that Portal also works as a good introduction to FPS games in general.

The game is fairly short, however I found that to be a strength compared to the second Portal game. I found the length and pacing to be just right, while Portal 2 felt like a bit of a drag at times. The mix of aesthetic, comedy and environmental storytelling all resulted in a very unique vibe that has not been recreated quite like that ever since.

I mean the game was alright, it was kinda short and while there were some challenging puzzles most of it was pretty easy (but I also haven't done the challenege/bonus maps). But the boss, i'm not going to lie, it was terrible, I was very dissapointed, it just wasn't that interesting.
It's definitely worth trying, but I wouldn't buy this game at full price, best to wait for a sale.
absolutely loved it, not too long and not too hard, i really liked the hidden rooms and my favorite part about it was the big room with a bunch of turrets

Portal the game series which exist in half life universe is an amazing concept . The game is very unique about  puzzle solving levels and it is very fantastic . I mean the game is one of the masterpiece games  valve had made after I have  played their  counter strike , half life , left 4 dead , etc . The storyline of the game is okay I can't say good or bad but you know valve is good at making fps games and does not focus on storyline most of the time except half life series. They left players to think about storyline and finding easter eggs , iceberg , myths , mystery, etc . That makes valve games so special and monopoly in the fps category which focuses on the perfection rather than failure . This game has no blood , no abusive language , no vulgar content , etc . The only thing this contain is puzzle solving with your brain and the proper use of portal gun also known as aperture science portal device . Purchase this portal  game series bundle  at the sale  which contain both portal 1 and 2 which cost me around 99 RS ,it's up to you . Solid puzzle solving game for me :)

My personal rating to this game-9.8/10 :)
Amazing puzzle game, amazing story, amazing voices, and the ending song is a masterpiece. Definitely worth the money, and the cake is NOT a lie!
A very solid puzzle game, one I can't believe took me this long to get to. Great the entire way through, worth a visit. It could stand to be longer, but maybe that's what Portal 2 is for.
ts was amazing first time playthrough and it still holds up well as an utterly unique and groundbreaking game.
Basically perfect. Install it again and spend a couple hours on it. Yes, again.

Jumped into Portal knowing nothing, and it's turned out to be one my favorite games.

I kept forgetting about the cameras every time I did a run for the Camera Shy achievement lol.

The cake is a lie!

A great game that can probably run on any device with a screen.

In case you play this and did not hate it outrageously you will worship Portal 2.

Yes. This game is older then me and I completed it (not counting the challenges) in less then 2 hours. But the entertainment value on a sale > 70% is a steal.
Also this games age makes it perfect to play on a typewriter.
Love the concept and the story. Makes you want to continue on. short game but its worth playing. If I was to buy the second game I had to of course buy the first for the story. I WANT MY CAKE!!!

a real 10/10 game

im extremely late to the party (is there still any cake left?) but this game really is a perfect experience neatly wrapped up into a few hours

if you haven't played it yet, you should

Portal is what I like about Valve games, it's full of life and people who clearly have a passion for writing stories and creating video games. I plan on %100'ing Portal and gaining all achievements. Valve is what all game studios want to be but can't, it's what makes them stand out and what makes me want to keep using Steam and playing their games. It makes me very GLaD...OS to be able to experience their games.

i didnt play half-life games

i dont have background about the story

i didnt know this game was made in 2007

and i am so late to play and finish this .. BUT

this is a must to play game i guess

for idea , for puzzles , for story ...

and there is version with RTX? worth replaying the game again

Do you believe that everything is because of a cake?
At this moment it doesn't really matter what I'll say. This game is legendary and everyone should atleast once complete it. I'm so proud of me for finishing this absolute masterpiece, in 100%.
Even though the campaign mode is very short, the constant renewal and evolution of the puzzles leave their iconic mark in the puzzle genre. 9/10.
Portal is a game that feels liminal and full of surprises either way a good pc game from valve and i recommend some one to play it
Very fun game that makes you think! I enjoyed that this game doesn't hold your hand and forces you to experiment. Game runs great with no issues with my 4080.

This is basically an Escape room puzzle game.
The Portal 'wormhole' mechanic makes seemingly impossible puzzles possible.
I find after a while of playing I sometime get a bit motion sick as your character can get twisted about when  jumping thought the portals.
Some bits require you create new portals while falling allowing for some crazy jumps to land you on a platform to progress. 
Some of the puzzles seem impossible, and others require you to be quick.  they are all possible.
The voice of the 'computer' GlaDos has several amusing quips, but is generally not very helpful.
Each level has diagrams on the walls from which you are supposed to gain clues on how to progress.
so basically you teleport in the game, through guess what... portals.

If you haven't played this game yet I highly recommend it. The puzzles are fun, challenging, and really make you think. I hate the least steps challenges with every fiber of my being. My recommendation for those is to save whenever you get anything done correctly so you don't spend hours upon hours trying to get it done in one clean run, it is not worth it. Still a good game though, 8/10.
great game. very innovative and it is so fun. it has this unsettling atmosphere that is just perfect, and the graphics are timeless. really makes you think
great game, just watched my friend beat it and he didn't take as long as me. i feel emasculatd.

One of if not the best puzzle game of all time. The portal gun is so fun to play with and the game itself is made for speedrunning. If you've got the time, Portal and Portal 2 as well are most surely masterpieces you should play.

The game was really good, but it needed more complex puzzles, more ideas, and stronger environmental variety to feel truly complete
8/10
Positively one of the best games I've ever played, so worth the 10 bucks. Deserves the hype.

For a game that came out 19 years ago, the visuals, sound effects, mechanics and puzzles are still some of the best out there.

I had to force myself to stop playing because with each level I kept saying "one more, how long could it possibly take" and an hour and a half later I finally managed to stop, right before starting level 17 out of 19. It pulls you in quickly and before you know it you're trying to figure out how to get to the next area and the next and the next, until you're all done. If I had started the next level I don't think I would have stopped until I finished the game.

The game starts you off very easy, just go from one end of the room to the other. You place your blue portal, go through, and the orange one that is connected to it will take you to your destination. As you go on the puzzles get more challenging, adding more and more steps to solving them, like putting cubes on buttons, using momentum, jumping over walls, and so on.

There are a few puzzles that are timed, but even with my slow reaction time I managed to do them very quickly, just needed to count the seconds the first time around. The only times I felt anxious were when the floor was made of sludge.

I feel like I'm the last person on this planet to play this game so I don't have anything new to say about it. I will say that I was fully expecting to feel motion sick from all the jumping between portals, but luckily it was not the case.

PROS:
- graphics hold up
- fun mechanics
- felt like a brain workout

CONS:
- the cake is a lie

This is the game that started it all.
A classic, there's nothing more to it.
It stands the test of time, and I loved playing it 20 years after it came out.

There are extremely few games that can boast not feeling dated 20 years later.

I 100% games and then rank them on a tierlist. I might start making guides for them too.
Portal is a very interesting game. I didn't really know A LOT about it before playing it, and the only stuff I did now was very meme-oriented.
As far as I can tell, Portal is probably one of the best puzzle games out there.
Not necessarily because it's "good", but rather because it was such a staple on the puzzle game genre and said genre exploded in popularity after this game released.
I don't know much about the Valve universe, so I can't really talk about the "lore implications" and such.
However, I am perfectly content in saying this was a fun 100% (except for the challenge levels) and it was a nice, neatly packed package. I still think it holds up to this day despite all the puzzles games I've played recently.

I've decided to remove my rankings on this here review. I'll add them personally some other time.
Really good game, highly recommend.

Portal is an amazing game. Not just for gameplay, but it’s in depth lore and also how it has evolved from what is was to what it became! To start off, the physics portion of the game, absolutely amazing, I admit there were a few minuscule areas where I couldn’t comprehend the portal mechanic, but that had to do with angles. Other than that it was actually very simple! Now, the lore, oh how deep it goes. Even the first game itself, it is fantastic! The way you slowly move through these sterile, yet worn and dirty chambers and later stumble across opened rooms filled with the Rattman’s ramblings is par for the course, then the climax where GLaDOS reveals what she did, why were the only human there, divine revelation! Lastly, what it was to what it became. I am very well known with how this started at as a game called Narbacular Drop, but how they built on that and created a masterpiece if beyond me!

All in all, Portal is amazing game. The techniques they teach you make sense, since they ramp it up like a test, starting with simple controls to move a box, to learning how to jump through a portal, fall and place another one to keep that momentum to fly through the portal you had just come out from is spectacular! 10/10! 5 stars!

THE CAKE IS A LIE
THE CAKE IS A LIE
THE CAKE IS A LIE

classic. great game, dont pass this one up.
really good game, love the story, also good game to speedrun

Best puzzle game ever made
Peak writing, peak liminal horror atmosphere. This game deserves way more credit than it gets.
I love how intense the ending is and how GLaDOS gets creepier and creepier as you remove her cores. The part where she betrays you made me panic as a kid. That turret section where you have to act fast was incredibly intense.
This game mogs Half-Life 2 and even its own sequel in how good it is

1000/10

Valse should make portal 3 instead of half life 3 and make it a horror game

Very fun and very funny. I loved the "speedy-thing goes in, speedy-thing comes out". I recommend to anyone who likes puzzle games. Not very hard and diffidently good to learn about puzzle games in general. Runs on low hardware as well.

Such a good game. puzzles are fun and can become challenging sometimes (only a few times)but the whole game is very enjoyable. story was surprisingly good i did not expect a good story and humorous character moments.

Portal was a game that has always caused my attention ever since I was really small just because from what I've seen from the surface it featured the type of robot designs I like. Now that I've finished the game, I love it a lot more than I thought I would, I appreciate the dark atmosphere throughout the game and the game being more than just "There's robots and portals."
Banger game. If you want to play any portal game, play portal 2 though. 9/10 game versus a 10/10 game :steam happy:
I like that this game still holds up to today. But after completing the advanced maps I have to eat cake to calm my nerves ;))

This game is nearly a masterpiece and i mean Nearly, because in pretty much every aspect it's over shadowed by it's sequel, but that's not particularly a bad thing, this game introduces so many cool ideas that it's no wounder the sequel is so good, all that's to say this game is awesome. would recommend :3
A masterpiece of game design and an unforgettable experience that everyone who's still alive should play.
this was one of the greatest games ive ever played, its short yeah but fantastic.

Portal is just a classic of a game. No two playthroughs are the same there are always little things that have not been noticed and the different achievements allow for extra hunting on new playthroughs.
Great game idk how many softlocks i got but idc its great deafeat glados while inhaling neurotoxin somehow i dont die instantly

This was the last game my brother got me before he died in a tragic car accident. Portal means the world to me as I was completely able to forget about him and just focus on the game. When I was at his funeral, I didn't even cry, I was just thinking "Man am I excited to go home and play some "Portal"". I then went home to play for about 5 hours before I ate some of my favorite fast food, "Long John Silvers".  I ordered the "3 Pc Chicken Meal", with a large fry and medium Coke Zero. It was so good then I remembered Portal and I was just so happy. Portal is such an awesome game and I hope more people with dead brothers like mine can join me and just truly appreciate this great game. Thank you sincerely, Valve, and Gabe Newell for making this astonishing game. - Vdash5, signing off..

For a 2007 puzzle game, the graphics are actually pretty good, and the story? Really well made, The story of a psycho robot throwing you in dangerous tests where you have to throw around high tech portals? Beautiful.

I'm very close to beating the game and i can't wait to play Portal 2, the story has to be even better.

Kudos VALVE, 10/10

An absolute classic.
Such a stellar time, with great puzzles amd humour.
This is one of those games everyone has to try, and something I strongly recommend.

Best puzzle type game I have ever played.
Looks so simple but really well made levels.
Also completed all the advanced levels, which were very tough to finish.
Fun game, I enjoy the puzzle solving and overall concept. The cake is a lie.

there are portals. yea. I played this game on a fking M1 mac mini so it is impossible to not play.


And GladOS is a bi-
The introduction of the portal series, and really just an opener to portal 2. Still a great game, especially for its time but nowhere near the amount of enjoyment as portal 2
i started playing these game after playing gravatas and it did not disappoint even after being this old of a game!

Portal is like a slice of cake, its short, sweet and NOT a lie. 
It has amazingly well done puzzles, with some top tier writing and a PHENOMENAL atmosphere. 
It is without a doubt one of Valves best games of all time.



...Also Still Alive is a banger, go listen to it.

i think the devs were too conscious of the First Player Portal Puzzle Experience because if you are new to portal i think it does a decent job of teaching you . i recall one room specifically ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ me up and my friends blamed the fact that it was old and it was portal 1 and not portal 2 but i enjoyed this game without looking anything up and i had a lot of fun with it

An absolute masterpiece that continues to stand the test of time in every conceivable way. The storytelling is brilliantly crafted, the visuals remain remarkably charming and distinctive, and the gameplay is as engaging and innovative today as it was when it first launched. 

Every chamber presents a fresh opportunity for creative thinking, experimentation, and satisfying problem-solving, making the entire experience feel endlessly rewarding. Few games achieve such a perfect balance of intelligence, humor, and design excellence.

At this point, all I want in life is a home AI assistant with GLaDOS's voice and charm.
Its a very tricky game but in a fun kind of way. I don't recommend playing for hours.
Games fun but is lacking content after about 100hrs but still is fun even with 350 hrs

Portal is a short and sweet puzzle platformer that will always be memorable for me. The puzzles themselves are tightly designed and fairly intuitive but the game itself is so short and I found them decently easy overall, always wanting more. What is really remarkable about this one however is how they Valve keeps you engaged through the whole experience with basically one voice carrying all the dialogue, a voice that is genuinely, timeless funny. The ending especially is a highlight of their amazing writing skills. Also, the atmosphere and visuals show their age a bit with the older Source Engine, but they still do exactly what they set out to, being beautifully creepy in this one.

This game can appeal to anyone, casuals, or more extreme players looking for a challenge with the bonus advanced courses. However, I think that the first experience through this timeless classic is truly something to be treasured. Again, despite being a 'puzzle-platformer' there is a deeper story to be told and that is truly the main draw.

This is yet another piece of Valve gaming history that you don't wanna miss out on.
[h2]Excellent; 9/10.[/h2]

rlly impressive game valve, despite the being that ts game is from 2007, also I found a skip on chamber 14 where i had to use the blue portal on the ground next to the rising platform, i got on the left section of chamber 14 and after the stairs dissembled itself, i used the orange portal where the stairs dissembled itself, and those portals launched me to the rising platform and the exit of chamber 14 (of course I'm not doing chamber 14 the normal way, also Glados looked pretty surprised as I did the skip for chamber 14. lol)
The story and world aren't as captivating as Portal 2, but if the benchmark is Portal 2, then nothing can be good. This game is fantastic.

I good introduction to the portal series. It feels like some guy had an idea about portal gun and made a game around it. Chill start up with a good story just like a cupcake. And The real cake being portal 2. recommend. 10/10
After almost 20 years I finally decided to play, and wow... what an experience. Very funny and cool lore, fun gameplay, a classic for sure! 10/10
just completed it :D it is very good game . but in the end, at some point i still had to look youtube for some tutorial :D

I bought this game 5 years ago, tried it, got too scared due to the VALVE intro and overall eerie atomosphere, and never ended up finishing it. 5 years later I finally came back to play it to completion, and it definitely lives up to its reputation and overwhemingly positive reviews. The game has simple puzzles, not too hard, but enough variation to keep it interesting. Small mechanics are introduced throughout, despite the game's short length. What I love though, is the robotic voice that comments on you, the humor is very entertaining and actually helped me complete it since I could attribute to the dreadful feeling of being watched to this eccentric AI. This game is a 10/10 in my book.
It's fun and will challenge your mind without it being so difficult cause there's no right answer to every questions. If it works for you, it works for you.
A classic. I love this game partially on nostalgia, but mostly for its own merits. It's portal. You already know
Insanity is doing the same thing over and over again and expecting different results - Mr Krabs
Easy to hard puzzles made perfectly, a wonderful story, its just THE perfect puzzle game.

Glad that I managed to get myself to actually play the game in one sitting after owning it for a while. Just a great game overall. I definitely would recommend this to other people, and I'll make sure to get the second one eventually.
Classic Value game with challenges and a good storyline. Portal will test your mind, simple yet challenging. 10/10 would play again.
This is an old game and there are many reviews. I knew the reputation of the game and it held up to the hype. Highly recommend!
Please play portal and portal 2 its so fun and its great and amazing so yeah play it yeah. Also its old so it'll run on whatever computer you have so thats great and amazing too

it's just good, also try portal 2, it's even better.

Probably runs well on POTaT OS.

A robot called me fat, science almost killed me repeatedly, and somehow I still felt emotionally supported the entire time. Quick playthrough but the end credit song is worth it.

The cake was a lie, but the experience was very real.

this game is the best game puzzle i ever played , the game play is unique the puzzle is mind blowing , i totally recommended this game for everyone .
10 / 10

I didn't play this when it originally came out, but it holds up very well even all these years later. You can finish this pretty easily in 4 hours, but it definitely has great replay potential so a solid price for the amount of content.

I wish I could play it again for the first time. Beat the game in 2 hours and not a single minute felt wasted. Game flows well and the puzzles are interesting and require some outside of the box thinking.

This puzzle game is way ahead of the time and it's still regarded as the best game ever made from the game company we all know and love. Got everything 100% completed and I'm having fun shooting portals blasting way out And listening GLaDOS.
(Someday we all remember this and laugh and laugh and laugh) :-D

Classic and never gets old! 10/10
it shouldve been longer but portal 2 does that job. either way, must-play

This is probably one of my favorite puzzle games ever. While yeah, it is pretty short, it was still a fun ride for me. The portal gun has got to be my  favorite gun in all of gaming, right next to the gravity gun from Half-Life 2.

10/10

words cannot describe how much fun and enjoyment I had when playing this game. 

It's the best.
Absolutely peak, Valve needs to make a third game based on Aperture's history with Black Mesa, or bring back Wheatley

Although old, this game stands on its own really well and ends of being one of the best puzzle games  to this date. GLaDOS is really funny and has amazing dialogue, the puzzles give you that "ah-ha"  moment in just the right way, the challenges provide an interesting way to play the game, and the base portal mechanic is perfect on its own.

Generational. Portal (along side half life) is my FAVORITE story game. no matter what PC you have it will run, it has GREAT lore, (not only that it has cake) and the ending is very cool! (which i will NOT spoil.) just and all around classic and great game! 
P.S the cake is a lie.

Although it's really short, it's also really fun especially if you like using your brain. The robot instructor GLaDOS is simultaneously scary, evil and hilarious. Also the turrets are funny and they have adorable voices / quips.

This was a triumph. I'm making a note here, huge success. It's hard to overstate my satisfaction.

I'm sure this joke has already been done to death. But I legitimately can't think of a better way to describe my feelings towards this game. Gaming empires will rise and fall, and Portal will continue to be one of my all-time favorites.

+good puzzles 
+nice atmosphere 
+really good physics engine 

-too short

I love the game and the song at the end!

One of the best games I've ever played in my life. This game is brilliant, everything in it is ahead of It's time (note game released, 2007). The portal gun mechanics are so fun to play around with, and the puzzles are so well made. Game had me hooked till the end 10/10 a must play!
short game but very cool and fun
